Vivere · cross-portfolio read

Where to focus this quarter

Two OpCos carry 72% of the Group's weighted at-risk load: MTN Nigeria (Customer Care & Network Ops) and MTN South Africa (Network Ops & Fintech agents). The shared driver across both is shift-based always-on customer signal. A coordinated cool-down pilot run jointly with both OpCo HR Directors would be the highest-leverage move available to you this quarter.

Aria Analytics · agent activity

How Aria is being used

Aria is the agent on the member side. This view tracks who's interacting with her, which features they engage, and how adoption tracks across the six OpCos. Vivere (your HR intelligence layer) reads this surface to ground every observation she shares with you.

Vivere · predictive scoring

Aria adoption · projected next quarter

Based on the current 8.6% week-over-week slope, I project Aria DAU reaching ~5,100 people by end of next quarter, with engagement rate stabilising near 58%. The lever that would most accelerate this: MTN Nigeria Network Ops onboarding (currently 41% adoption — Group lagger). Confidence: 74%.

⊕ Connected apps earn up to +200 kudos/day from MTN apps kudos arrive monthly · via MoMo from department

Aria · how kudos actually work Kudos are earned only inside MTN — from honouring my wellbeing nudges (taking a walk, holding focus time, declining a meeting that's costing you), from MTN's own apps (MTN Move, Library, Audio books, Meditation Room, Garden Pavilion, Prayer rooms), and from using your leave. Your department also pays a monthly kudos baseline straight into your MoMo wallet — you can let them accumulate, spend them at WiCode partners below, or swap them to rand cash with a R1 transfer fee. Current balance: 45. Want me to show what's worth spending it on?

Privacy Your individual scores are visible only to you. Everything shown at function or OpCo level is aggregated across a minimum cohort of 10 people, so no single person is identifiable. Your manager sees the function trend — never your individual numbers.
Action log · everything Aria has done
Every action Aria takes is logged here with what was done, when, and that you authorized it. This is your audit trail — nothing happens without your say-so.

Aria is ready when you are

Beginning, Bigger Vision

Aria is your AI designed to understand you. Think, plan, and protect your week effortlessly. This helps you reflect, focus, and make wellbeing choices smarter — all in one place.

Wellbeing programme active for MTN · Open Today

We're shaping the future together. Stay ready — Thrive AI is the wellbeing companion powered by Aria and built for human pace.

Theme palette
Pick the colour palette for the whole platform. Time-aware features (morning/evening labels, sky hero) still follow the clock; only colours change here.

Yellow

Appearance
Time & thematic scheduler Signature
Lets the interface follow the time of day — sunrise warmth in the morning, deep tones at night. Turn off to keep a constant default Aria look.
Reduce motion
Dial down animations and transitions across the surface.
Aria
Aria nudges
Show contextual nudges across your Today surface — lunch reminders, focus blocks, prep cards.
Focus mode (global)
Mute Teams, set Outlook auto-reply, hold notifications to Critical-only. Stays on across sessions until you turn it off here.
Sound cues
A soft tone when an action is authorized. Off by default.
Privacy
Aggregate-only sharing Locked
Your individual scores are never shared. Managers see function-level aggregates of 10+ people only. This protection can't be disabled.
Display & zoom
UI zoom
Scale the entire interface up or down. Applies instantly. 100%
Compact mode
Tighter spacing throughout. Useful on smaller screens or when you want more density.
Force mobile layout
Stack everything to a single column with a slide-in nav. Auto-on under 768 px.
Companion tabs
Hide tabs you don't use. They'll disappear from Vivere's rail.
Workspace sections
Hide sections from your main view. Reorder coming soon.
Changes apply immediately. In this prototype they reset on sign-out.

+3 vs your team
Team size
220
team you sit in
How the team is doing — vs your scores
Aggregate onlyThis is your team seen as a whole, never as individuals — no single person's data is visible here, not yours and not a colleague's. Your numbers appear in the "Your wellbeing" card so you can see where you sit relative to the team's average. The 10-person rule: if a team or function has fewer than 10 people, the platform won't show its breakdown at all — too small a group risks identifying individuals from their scores. That protection holds across every aggregate view on the platform.

Setting up the test bench…
Eye health, brain reflex, and a one-minute wellbeing check-in.
Take a test
Aria · 20-20-20 screen time check
You've been at your screen for 47 minutes since your last break. The 20-20-20 rule — every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (6m) away for 20 seconds — is the optometry-recommended baseline. These checks aren't earnings, they're checks. Not medical advice — if anything's persistent, speak to your doctor or optometrist. Mental check-ins are powered by Sentimeter; eye and awareness games are powered by MTN Move.
Test bed · today
7 simulations available
3
Eye checks
3
Mind checks
1
Reflex
20·20·20
screen rule
👁 Eye health 3 simulations · powered by MTN Move
◐ Mental wellness 3 simulations · powered by Sentimeter
🧠 Reflex & awareness 1 simulation · powered by MTN Move
📖 Research basis · why these tests are here

The mental-wellness games are informed by Naboo's 2024 review of 20 essential games for team mental health — which found that low-effort, brief cognitive games (word puzzles, memory tasks, reflection prompts) during the workday reduce reported stress by 18% and improve self-reported focus by 23% over a four-week cycle. The 5 Cs reflection card maps to Horton International's 5 Cs of Mental Health framework (Competence, Confidence, Connection, Character, Caring) — the same dimensions used by occupational psychologists in workplace wellness audits.

The eye tests use clinically standard targets: a Snellen chart (acuity), the radiating-line astigmatism test, and Ishihara dot plates for red-green colour vision. These don't replace an optometrist — they flag patterns over time. If something stays off for two weeks, that's a conversation to have with a professional.

Sources: Naboo · 20 mental-health games for workplaces (2024) · Horton International · The 5 Cs of Mental Health (2023) · Snellen visual acuity standard · Ishihara colour vision plates (1917)
Powered by MTN Move

Eye Health Update

Powered by Horton International · 5 Cs framework

The 5 Cs of Mental Health

A quick reflection across the five dimensions psychologists use in workplace wellness audits. Rate yourself 1–5 on each. No-one else sees this — it's between you and Aria.
